## Knowledge Base

### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)

- **Binary-product shear-iso closure recipe**
  *(NEW iter-134 Archon canonical — surfaced + codified by the iter-134
  prover lane on `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.shearMulRight` at
  `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:349`)*:
  When closing the inverse-pair `hom_inv_id` / `inv_hom_id` proofs of a
  binary-product shear iso `σ : G ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ G` in a Cartesian monoidal
  category `C` over `GrpObj G` data (with `σ.hom := lift (fst G G) μ`,
  `σ.inv := lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ)`), the
  Mathlib template `CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight`'s bulk `simp_only`
  chain DOES NOT transfer — the binary-product version has 2 free
  inputs (`fst`, `snd`) where `mulRight` has 1, so `comp_lift_assoc`
  matches too eagerly and the goal stays out of `lift_fst_snd`-reducible
  form. Recipe that works:
  ```lean
  apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext
  · simp                                                 -- fst coord
  · rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, lift_snd]
    rw [← Category.assoc, comp_lift]
    rw [← Category.assoc, lift_fst, lift_snd]
    -- Goal: `lift (fst G G ≫ ι) μ ≫ μ = snd G G`
    -- (or symmetric `lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ) ≫ μ = snd G G`
    --  for inv_hom_id; positions of `μ` and `ι` flip)
    rw [show (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) μ ≫ μ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G) =
        lift (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (fst G G) ≫ μ) (snd G G) ≫ μ from by
          rw [MonObj.lift_lift_assoc, lift_fst_snd, Category.id_comp],
      CategoryTheory.GrpObj.lift_comp_inv_left,           -- or `lift_comp_inv_right`
      MonObj.lift_comp_one_left]
  ```
  **Key insight**: structural rewrites (`Category.id_comp`, `lift_snd`,
  `comp_lift`, `lift_fst`) must precede the `lift_lift_assoc` rewrite,
  AND the inner `μ`-expansion to `lift (fst G G) (snd G G) ≫ μ` must
  happen at exactly the right pattern position via a single `show`-
  equation (NOT `conv_lhs`, which matches both occurrences of `μ`).
  This NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL contribution candidate (Mathlib has no
  packaged binary-product shear iso) can be upstreamed once stable. ~30
  LOC per inverse-pair, kernel-only axioms.

- **The `Nonempty (X ≅ X)` tautological-placeholder anti-pattern (DO NOT REPEAT)**
  *(NEW iter-134 Archon canonical NEGATIVE finding — surfaced by both
  `lean-auditor-review134` and
  `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-cotangent-grpobj-review134` as
  must-fix-this-iter on 3 load-bearing declarations in
  `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:476/508/566`)*:
  When the prover lane needs to land a named declaration for a
  multi-iter Mathlib-gap target (e.g., a deferred sheaf-level base-change
  iso), **DO NOT** ship the body as
  `theorem foo : Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩` with the intended
  substantive type recorded only in the docstring. This pattern:
  - Compiles cleanly with kernel-only axioms (because `Iso.refl _` is
    a real term, not `sorry`).
  - Emits **zero machine-readable incompleteness signal** —
    `sorry_analyzer`, `lean_diagnostic_messages`, and `sync_leanok` all
    classify the declaration as complete; the deterministic
    `\leanok`-sync phase will add `\leanok` to the blueprint's proof
    block because the Lean compiles.
  - Triggers the strict rubric of `lean-auditor` + `lean-vs-blueprint-checker`
    as "weakened-wrong-definition + signature mismatch + excuse-comment"
    on the load-bearing declaration. `\notready` on the blueprint side
    + intended-type prose in the docstring **does not** clear the
    rubric.
  - Leaves the dependency surface broken: parameters like
    `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`, `[IsProper G.hom]`,
    `[GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]` declared on the placeholder are
    **unused** by `Iso.refl _`, a Lean-side smoke signal of the
    body/type mismatch (Lean's unused-variable hints fire under strict
    settings).
  **PREFERRED PATTERN** when a multi-iter Mathlib-gap target needs a
  scaffold: write the **intended** type in the signature and ship the
  body as `sorry`. Mirrors `Jacobian.lean:194-215`'s
  `positiveGenusWitness` honest scaffold (audited iter-134 by
  `lean-auditor-review134` and classified as honest). Example, taking
  `mulRight_globalises_cotangent` from iter-134:
  ```lean
  -- BAD (iter-134 anti-pattern):
  theorem mulRight_globalises_cotangent (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) ... :
      Nonempty (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom ≅
                Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom) :=
    ⟨Iso.refl _⟩

  -- GOOD (honest scaffold):
  noncomputable def mulRight_globalises_cotangent
      (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) ... :
      Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom ≅
        (PresheafOfModules.pullback (φ_str G)).obj
          ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (φ_η G)).obj
            (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom)) :=
    sorry
  ```
  The honest scaffold:
  - Emits a downstream-visible `sorry` (sorry counter increments).
  - Pins the intended signature for the iter-135+ prover lane to fill in.
  - Uses the declared instance parameters (because the intended type
    references them through `PresheafOfModules.pullback (φ_str G)`).
  - Aligns with the project's iter-127-onward scaffold convention
    (`genusZeroWitness` iter-127, `nonempty_jacobianWitness` Phase-C,
    `rigidity_over_kbar` iter-126, `positiveGenusWitness` iter-134).
  **Codified rule**: NEVER ship `Nonempty (X ≅ X)` as a stand-in for a
  substantive iso. If the iter cannot produce the intended type
  signature (because the supporting infrastructure — e.g. `φ_*`
  compatibility morphisms — does not yet exist), then either (a)
  produce the supporting infrastructure in the same iter, or (b) DEFER
  the declaration entirely until the iter that can produce it. Do not
  introduce a hollow placeholder declaration into the environment.

- **Plan-phase deepening over low-quality prover dispatch when a HARD GATE fires**

324	  Let $\mu \colon G \times_{k} G \to G$ denote the multiplication of $G$ and let $\sigma \colon G \times_{k} G \to G \times_{k} G$ be the \emph{shear isomorphism}
325	  \[
326	    \sigma \;=\; \langle \pr_{1},\, \mu \rangle, \qquad (a, b) \;\mapsto\; (a,\, a \cdot b),
327	  \]
328	  built categorically from $\mu$ and the binary-product projections in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$. Then $\sigma$ is an isomorphism over the first projection, and pullback along $\sigma$ together with the canonical first-projection-cotangent identification $\Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G} \cong \pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$ (\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj}) yields a natural isomorphism of presheaves of modules on $G$
329	  \[
330	    \Omega_{G/k} \;\cong\; \pi_{G}^{*}\!\bigl(\eta_{G}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}\bigr) \qquad \text{on } G,
331	  \]
332	  where $\pi_{G} \colon G \to \Spec k$ is the structure map (in the standard category-theory notation for the binary product in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$, this is the "first projection" of the singleton-product-with-base, sometimes written $\pr_{1}$; we use $\pi_{G}$ to avoid confusion with the binary-product first projection $\pr_{1} \colon G \times_{k} G \to G$ appearing above). Informally: the relative cotangent of $G$ is canonically isomorphic to the constant $\mathcal O_{G}$-module with fibre $\eta_{G}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$, trivialised along the structure map.
333	
334	  \paragraph{Iter-127 over-k risk register.} The globalisation \textbf{must} be formulated functorially via the shear scheme map $\sigma$ — which is a morphism in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$, valid for any base field — and \textbf{not} as a pointwise translation by closed (or $\bar k$-rational) points of $G$. The pointwise-translation argument requires either alg-closure of $k$ or a Nullstellensatz-style $\bar k$-point lifting (as in Mathlib's \texttt{pointEquivClosedPoint}, used in the alg-closed-base branch of \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth.lean:50--51}); the functorial shear formulation sidesteps that obstacle entirely. The iter-133 mathlib-analogist verdict (\texttt{analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md} Decision 4) explicitly rules out the pointwise idioms of \texttt{Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.GroupLieAlgebra.mulInvariantVectorField} (requires \texttt{[Group G]}) and \texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Algebra.Group.Basic.Homeomorph.shearMulRight} (requires \texttt{[Group G]} on the carrier) as the over-k-forbidden alternatives.
335	
336	  \paragraph{Lean target name vs.\ construction (option (i), iter-133).} The named target \texttt{mulRight\_globalises\_cotangent} retains the \texttt{mulRight} naming convention because the shear iso $\sigma = \langle \pr_{1}, \mu \rangle$ on $G \times_{k} G$ is the binary-product upgrade of \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight} (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp\_.lean:277--281}, which packages a one-input automorphism $a \mapsto a \cdot f$ for a fixed global element $f \colon \mathbf{1} \to G$): the map $\sigma$ parametrises the family of right-translations $\{\mathrm{mulRight}\,a\}_{a \in G}$ globally across $G$ by taking the first coordinate as the parameter, and the second projection $\pr_{2} \circ \sigma = \mu$ realises the right-translation action on the second coordinate. The "globalises the cotangent" phrasing in the name records that this family-of-translations is what triviallises $\Omega_{G/k}$ via pullback along $\sigma$ (cf.\ the inverse-pair proof shape of \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.isPullback}, \texttt{Grp\_.lean:293--323}, which uses the structurally identical \texttt{lift \dots (lift \dots \,\iota\, \dots) ≫ μ} construction). No standalone \texttt{mulRight}-name function appears in the Lean signature itself; the connection is purely nomenclatural, and the underlying Lean construction is the binary-product shear iso, not a one-input \texttt{mulRight} automorphism.

View $G \times_{k} G$ as a $G$-scheme via $\pr_{1}$. Then there is a canonical natural isomorphism of presheaves of modules on $G \times_{k} G$
420	  \[
421	    \Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G} \;\cong\; \pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k},
422	  \]
423	  where the left-hand side is the relative cotangent of $G \times_{k} G$ viewed as a $G$-scheme via $\pr_{1}$, and the right-hand side is the pullback along $\pr_{2}$ of $\Omega_{G/k} = \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}\,G.\mathrm{hom}$.

427	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
428	  \leanok
429	  The construction chains two Mathlib idioms. On each affine chart $W = \Spec B$ of the source $G \times_{k} G$, the sections of both sides are Kähler-differential modules with appropriate base rings. The left-hand side $\Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G}$ over $W$ gives $\Omega_{B/B_{1}}$, where $B_{1}$ corresponds to the chart of $G$ obtained by restricting $\pr_{1}$ to $W$. The right-hand side $\pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$ over $W$ gives $B \otimes_{B_{2}} \Omega_{B_{2}/k}$, where $B_{2}$ corresponds to the chart of $G$ obtained by restricting $\pr_{2}$. The binary-product universal property in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$ supplies an \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout}\,$k$\,$B_{1}$\,$B_{2}$\,$B$ square (the two projections $B_{1}, B_{2} \to B$ exhibit $B$ as the tensor product $B_{1} \otimes_{k} B_{2}$ at chart level). The algebra-side base-change identity
430	  \[
431	    \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv} \colon \;\; B \,\otimes_{B_{2}}\, \Omega_{B_{2}/k} \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\; \Omega_{B/B_{1}}
432	  \]
433	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.TensorProduct}; for an \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout R S A B} square, yields $B \otimes_{A} \Omega[A/R] \simeq_{B} \Omega[B/S]$) gives the chart-level value identity. Promoting this chart-by-chart equivalence to a natural iso of presheaves of modules uses the presheaf-side functor \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} (\texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Pullback}) composed with the project-side chart-level value identity \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_obj\_kaehler} (\cref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}, \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean:60}); naturality follows from naturality of \texttt{tensorKaehlerEquiv} and of \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback}.
434	
435	  \emph{Mathlib name summary (NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL, $\sim 150$--$300$ LOC).} Algebra-side: \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.TensorProduct}). Presheaf-side: \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} (\texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Pullback}) and \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback} (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pullback}). Project-side: \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_obj\_kaehler} (\cref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}, \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean:60--66}). This is the load-bearing piece of (i.b); without it, the shear iso of Step 1 of \cref{lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises}'s proof cannot be turned into a cotangent-level iso. Mathlib has no scheme-level relative cotangent sheaf at all (per \texttt{analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md} Decision 2), so this lemma is a genuine NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL contribution candidate. Compatible upstream Mathlib PR-shape: a sheaf-of-modules version of \texttt{tensorKaehlerEquiv} promoting \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout}-of-charts to a presheaf-level natural iso.
436	\end{proof}
